snippets.title: Discrete KL divergence from scratch
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def kl_divergence(p, q, eps=1e-12):
    """D_KL(P || Q) for discrete PMFs p, q (same support)."""
    p = np.asarray(p, dtype=float)
    q = np.asarray(q, dtype=float)
    p = p / p.sum()
    q = np.clip(q, eps, None)
    q = q / q.sum()
    return np.sum(p * np.log(p / q))

p = np.array([0.5, 0.5])       # fair coin
q = np.array([0.9, 0.1])       # biased coin
print("D_KL(P || Q):", kl_divergence(p, q))
print("D_KL(Q || P):", kl_divergence(q, p))
snippets.explanation: Computes D_KL(P || Q) = Σ p log(p/q). Clipping q avoids log(0) when supports match; true support mismatch should yield infinity, not a silent finite number.

snippets.title: KL via cross-entropy minus entropy
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def cross_entropy(p, q, eps=1e-12):
    p = np.asarray(p, dtype=float)
    q = np.clip(np.asarray(q, dtype=float), eps, None)
    p, q = p / p.sum(), q / q.sum()
    return -np.sum(p * np.log(q))

def entropy(p, eps=1e-12):
    p = np.asarray(p, dtype=float)
    p = p / p.sum()
    p = np.clip(p, eps, None)
    return -np.sum(p * np.log(p))

p = np.array([0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25])
q = np.array([0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1])
print("H(P,Q) - H(P):", cross_entropy(p, q) - entropy(p))
snippets.explanation: Verifies the identity D_KL(P || Q) = H(P, Q) − H(P). Useful when cross-entropy is already implemented in a training loop.

snippets.title: PyTorch KL on categorical distributions
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

# log-probabilities from model; target one-hot or soft labels
log_q = F.log_softmax(torch.tensor([2.0, 1.0, 0.1]), dim=0)
target = torch.tensor([1.0, 0.0, 0.0])

# batchmean reduction: average KL over batch
kl = F.kl_div(log_q, target, reduction="batchmean")
print("KL (nats):", kl.item())
snippets.explanation: `F.kl_div` expects log-probabilities for the model side and probabilities for the target. Check PyTorch docs for `reduction` semantics when batching.
